title: Session registry with atomic file operations and backup recovery
purpose: Manages persistent session records for projects, ensuring atomic writes with backup recovery and validating session directory existence.
module_context: Exports a SessionRegistry class with singleton instance and custom error type for registry corruption. Includes helper methods for file system operations and session management.

methods.name: SessionRegistryCorruptError
methods.normalized_params: (registryPath: string, backupPath: string, cause?: unknown): void

steps.content: Construct an error indicating sessions.json is corrupt and recovery failed.
Set the error name to 'SessionRegistryCorruptError'.
Store the registry path and backup path for operator reference.
Initialize a mutex with a promise chain to serialize asynchronous operations.
Acquire the mutex by chaining the function to the existing promise chain.
Await the previous operation in the chain to ensure sequential execution.
Execute the provided function and return its result.
Release the mutex by resolving the next promise in the chain.
Initialize the session registry with a specified path or default.
Set up a mutex for serializing write operations within a single process.
Return the path to the backup file by appending '.bak' to the registry path.
Return the path to the temporary file by appending '.tmp' to the registry path.
Check if the specified file path exists.
IF the file does not exist, create the directory structure recursively.
IF the file does not exist, write the specified content to the file.
Parse the JSON content into an object.
Validate that the parsed object has a sessions array.
Cast the parsed object to the expected registry data type.
Check if the registry file exists.
IF the file does not exist, return an empty session registry.
IF the file exists, attempt to read and parse its content.
IF reading fails due to ENOENT, return an empty session registry.
IF reading fails due to corruption, attempt to recover from the backup file.
IF recovery from backup fails, throw a SessionRegistryCorruptError.
Ensure the directory for the registry file exists.
Write the registry data to a temporary file.
Sync the temporary file to disk to ensure durability.
If the registry file exists, rename it to the backup path.
Rename the temporary file to the registry file path to complete the atomic write.
IF the final rename fails, attempt to clean up the temporary file.

IF the session exists, update its last access timestamp.
IF the session does not exist, add it to the registry.
Save the updated registry to disk.
Ensure the session directory structure exists for the new session.
Create a collab-state.json file for the session if it does not exist.

IF stale sessions are found, acquire the write mutex to clean them.
Re-load the registry under the mutex to avoid racing with other writers.
Filter out stale sessions from the registry.
Save the cleaned registry to disk.
Sort valid sessions by last access time descending.

Validate that the type is one of the allowed values.
Check if the new session path exists.

Return the path that exists or the default new path.
